title: Fix RFC 8613 nonce and option encoding
description: Rust OSCORE nonce construction XORs Sender ID length with PIV length instead of encoding Sender ID length, no-PIV responses use responder identity instead of the request's complete nonce, empty response OSCORE options encode as 0x00 instead of empty, and parser accepts reserved bits/trailing bytes. Correct these RFC 8613 interoperability defects with independent published-vector or literal formula tests.
acceptance_criteria: Nonce formula matches RFC 8613 Section 5.2 literals; no-PIV response reuses request nonce identity; empty option is zero bytes; reserved bits 5-7 and trailing bytes with k=0 reject; existing RFC vectors and roundtrips pass; no_std/release/all-feature tests, Clippy, and three reviews pass.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with RFC-literal tests; debug/release/all-feature/no-default suites and library Clippy pass; three hash-bound reviews found no remaining in-scope P0-P2 defects. Context lifecycle remains .48.2.
